buckethost 0.2.1

    • Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
  • Changelog

Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/

Source: R/url.R
bucketRaster.Rd

Convenience wrapper around terra::rast("/vsicurl/<url>"). When the object is a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, GDAL fetches only the byte ranges needed — opening metadata or cropping to a small area transfers a few hundred KB rather than the whole file.

Usage

bucketRaster(path, container = NULL, endpoint = NULL, ...)

Arguments

path

Object key relative to the bucket root, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif".

container

The bucket (container) name. When NULL (the default), resolved from options(buckethost.container=), then BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, then a built-in default.

endpoint

The object store's HTTPS host (no bucket name). When NULL, resolved from options(buckethost.endpoint=), then BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, then a built-in default.

...

Passed to terra::rast().

Value

A terra::SpatRaster.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
r <- bucketRaster("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif")
r                                  # metadata only, no full download
terra::crop(r, my_aoi)             # fetches just the AOI's bytes
} # }

On this page
